title: Launch Your AI Side Project

modules.title: Why Most Side Projects Die and How Agile Fixes That
modules.sub: Diagnose the real reasons your ideas stall out
modules.description: Learners examine the common failure patterns that kill side projects before they ship, from endless planning to tool overload. They're introduced to the core principles of agile and why the framework works especially well for solo builders with limited time and resources.

modules.title: Scoping Your Idea: Using AI to Go from Vague to Viable
modules.sub: Pressure-test your concept before writing a single line
modules.description: Learners use AI tools to clarify a fuzzy idea into a focused, buildable concept with a defined audience and a single core problem to solve. They produce a one-page project brief that sets the boundaries of their first sprint and prevents early scope blowout.

modules.title: Sprint Cycles for Solo Builders: Ship in Weeks, Not Months
modules.sub: Design a personal sprint rhythm that actually fits your life
modules.description: Learners map out a realistic sprint structure tailored to part-time builders, including how to set sprint goals, define done, and keep momentum without a team. They walk away with a repeatable weekly rhythm they can apply immediately to their own project.

modules.title: AI Tools for Every Stage: Research, Build, Test, Repeat
modules.sub: Match the right AI tool to each phase of your workflow
modules.description: Learners take a guided tour through a curated stack of AI tools organized by project stage: discovery, building, testing, and iteration. They practice slotting tools into their own sprint plan so that every phase has clear AI-assisted support rather than random tool-hopping.

modules.title: Handling Scope Creep When AI Makes Everything Feel Possible
modules.sub: Stay focused when AI keeps opening new doors
modules.description: Learners identify the specific ways AI accelerates scope creep by making new features and ideas feel cheap and fast to build. They practice a decision filter for evaluating new ideas mid-sprint, learning to log them without letting them derail current progress.

modules.title: Getting Real Feedback Fast: Users, Iteration, and Pivots
modules.sub: Put your work in front of real people and act on what you hear
modules.description: Learners design a lightweight feedback loop to get genuine responses from real users even before a product feels polished or complete. They use AI to synthesize feedback, identify patterns, and decide whether to iterate on their current direction or make a deliberate pivot.

modules.title: Turning a Side Project into a Portfolio Piece or Income Stream
modules.sub: Decide what your project is worth and to whom
modules.description: Learners evaluate their completed project against two paths: showcasing it as a professional portfolio piece or positioning it as a product with revenue potential. They use AI to draft the assets needed for either path, including case study write-ups, landing page copy, or a basic monetization plan.

modules.title: Your First Sprint: Plan and Launch Something in 30 Days
modules.sub: Commit to a real sprint and ship something you're proud of
modules.description: Learners put everything together by building a complete 30-day sprint plan for their own side project, with weekly milestones, tool assignments, and a defined launch target. This capstone module is action-oriented: the deliverable is not a plan on paper but a project actually set in motion.

synopsis: You've got an idea and a handful of AI tools â but most side projects stall before they get anywhere real. This course teaches you a lightweight agile workflow designed specifically for solo builders and small teams using AI, so you can stop planning and start shipping.
rationale: Young adults are increasingly using AI to build side projects but lack the project management frameworks to take them from idea to completion, leading to high abandonment rates and missed skill-building opportunities.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can run a structured agile sprint using AI tools to take a side project from initial idea to a working, shareable version within 30 days.
source_signals: Ex-GitHub CEO launches a new developer platform for AI agents
